title: What is a MySQL covering index?
description: What is a covering index?
options: An index that contains all columns needed by a query so MySQL reads the result entirely from the index without accessing the table data (shows 'Using index' in EXPLAIN)
correct_answer: An index that contains all columns needed by a query so MySQL reads the result entirely from the index without accessing the table data (shows 'Using index' in EXPLAIN)

title: What is the difference between CHAR and VARCHAR?
description: When should you use CHAR vs VARCHAR?
options: CHAR is fixed-length (padded with spaces, faster for fixed-size data); VARCHAR is variable-length (uses 1-2 bytes for length prefix, space-efficient for varying data)
correct_answer: CHAR is fixed-length (padded with spaces, faster for fixed-size data); VARCHAR is variable-length (uses 1-2 bytes for length prefix, space-efficient for varying data)

title: What is MySQL partitioning?
description: How does table partitioning work?
options: Divides a table into smaller physical pieces by range, list, hash, or key while appearing as one logical table; improves query performance via partition pruning and enables easier data management
correct_answer: Divides a table into smaller physical pieces by range, list, hash, or key while appearing as one logical table; improves query performance via partition pruning and enables easier data management

title: What is the difference between TRUNCATE and DELETE?
description: How do TRUNCATE and DELETE differ?
options: DELETE removes rows one by one, logs each deletion, can use WHERE, fires triggers, and can be rolled back; TRUNCATE drops and recreates the table, is faster, resets AUTO_INCREMENT, cannot use WHERE
correct_answer: DELETE removes rows one by one, logs each deletion, can use WHERE, fires triggers, and can be rolled back; TRUNCATE drops and recreates the table, is faster, resets AUTO_INCREMENT, cannot use WHERE

title: What is a MySQL materialized view workaround?
description: How do you simulate materialized views in MySQL?
options: MySQL doesn't support materialized views natively; simulate using a separate table populated by INSERT...SELECT, refreshed via scheduled events or triggers
correct_answer: MySQL doesn't support materialized views natively; simulate using a separate table populated by INSERT...SELECT, refreshed via scheduled events or triggers

title: What is the difference between ENUM and SET type?
description: How do ENUM and SET differ?
options: ENUM allows exactly one value from a predefined list; SET allows zero or more values from the list stored as a bitmap. Both are stored as integers internally
correct_answer: ENUM allows exactly one value from a predefined list; SET allows zero or more values from the list stored as a bitmap. Both are stored as integers internally

title: What is the MySQL query cache?
description: How did the query cache work and why was it removed?
options: Cached SELECT results keyed by exact query string; invalidated entire cache for a table on any write. Removed in MySQL 8.0 because it caused contention and scaling issues; modern alternatives: ProxySQL, application caching
correct_answer: Cached SELECT results keyed by exact query string; invalidated entire cache for a table on any write. Removed in MySQL 8.0 because it caused contention and scaling issues; modern alternatives: ProxySQL, application caching

title: Write a query to find employees hired in the last 30 days per department.
description: Count recent hires by department.
correct_answer:
SELECT department, COUNT(*) AS recent_hires
FROM employees
WHERE hire_date >= DATE_SUB(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 30 DAY)
GROUP BY department
ORDER BY recent_hires DESC;

title: Write a query using recursive CTE to generate a number sequence.
description: Generate numbers 1 to 100 using recursive CTE.

title: What is the difference between InnoDB and MyISAM?
description: Compare InnoDB and MyISAM storage engines.
options: InnoDB: ACID compliant, row-level locking, supports transactions and foreign keys, crash-safe. MyISAM: table-level locking, no transactions, faster for read-heavy workloads with full-text search (legacy); InnoDB is default since MySQL 5.5
correct_answer: InnoDB: ACID compliant, row-level locking, supports transactions and foreign keys, crash-safe. MyISAM: table-level locking, no transactions, faster for read-heavy workloads with full-text search (legacy); InnoDB is default since MySQL 5.5

title: What is the MySQL slow query log?
description: How do you use the slow query log?
options: Records queries exceeding long_query_time threshold; enable with SET GLOBAL slow_query_log = 'ON'; analyze with mysqldumpslow or pt-query-digest to find optimization targets
correct_answer: Records queries exceeding long_query_time threshold; enable with SET GLOBAL slow_query_log = 'ON'; analyze with mysqldumpslow or pt-query-digest to find optimization targets

title: Write a query to implement pagination with cursors.
description: Implement cursor-based pagination.
correct_answer:
-- Cursor-based pagination (more efficient than OFFSET for large datasets)
SELECT id, name, created_at
FROM products
WHERE id > :last_seen_id  -- cursor from previous page
ORDER BY id ASC
LIMIT 20;

title: What is the difference between ON DELETE CASCADE and ON DELETE SET NULL?
description: Compare foreign key delete actions.
options: CASCADE automatically deletes child rows when parent is deleted; SET NULL sets the foreign key column to NULL (column must be nullable). Other options: RESTRICT (prevent), NO ACTION (same as RESTRICT), SET DEFAULT
correct_answer: CASCADE automatically deletes child rows when parent is deleted; SET NULL sets the foreign key column to NULL (column must be nullable). Other options: RESTRICT (prevent), NO ACTION (same as RESTRICT), SET DEFAULT

title: Write a query to find overlapping date ranges.
description: Find records with overlapping date ranges.
correct_answer:
SELECT a.id AS id1, b.id AS id2, a.start_date, a.end_date, b.start_date, b.end_date
FROM bookings a
JOIN bookings b ON a.id < b.id
  AND a.room_id = b.room_id
  AND a.start_date < b.end_date
  AND b.start_date < a.end_date;

title: What is a MySQL event scheduler?
description: How does the MySQL event scheduler work?
options: Runs scheduled SQL tasks (like cron); CREATE EVENT runs statements at intervals or specific times; enable with SET GLOBAL event_scheduler = ON; useful for cleanup, aggregations, and maintenance
correct_answer: Runs scheduled SQL tasks (like cron); CREATE EVENT runs statements at intervals or specific times; enable with SET GLOBAL event_scheduler = ON; useful for cleanup, aggregations, and maintenance

title: Write a query to detect and remove orphaned records.
description: Find child records whose parent no longer exists.
correct_answer:
-- Find orphaned records
SELECT c.*
FROM order_items c
LEFT JOIN orders p ON c.order_id = p.id
WHERE p.id IS NULL;

-- Remove orphans
DELETE c FROM order_items c
LEFT JOIN orders p ON c.order_id = p.id
WHERE p.id IS NULL;

title: What is the difference between NATURAL JOIN and USING?
description: Compare NATURAL JOIN with JOIN...USING.
options: NATURAL JOIN automatically joins on all columns with matching names (fragile, can break if columns are added); USING(col) explicitly specifies which columns to join on. USING is safer and preferred
correct_answer: NATURAL JOIN automatically joins on all columns with matching names (fragile, can break if columns are added); USING(col) explicitly specifies which columns to join on. USING is safer and preferred

title: Write a query to calculate median salary.
description: Calculate the median of a column.
correct_answer:
SELECT AVG(salary) AS median_salary
FROM (
  SELECT salary, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY salary) AS rn,
         COUNT(*) OVER () AS total
  FROM employees
) sub
WHERE rn IN (FLOOR((total + 1) / 2), CEIL((total + 1) / 2));

title: What is MySQL replication?
description: How does MySQL replication work?
options: Source server writes changes to binary log; replica reads and replays them. Types: async (default), semi-sync, group replication. Modes: statement-based, row-based, mixed. Used for read scaling, backups, HA
correct_answer: Source server writes changes to binary log; replica reads and replays them. Types: async (default), semi-sync, group replication. Modes: statement-based, row-based, mixed. Used for read scaling, backups, HA

title: Write a query to find the longest consecutive streak.
description: Find the longest consecutive daily login streak per user.
correct_answer:
WITH grouped AS (
  SELECT user_id, login_date,
    DATE_SUB(login_date, INTERVAL ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY login_date) DAY) AS grp
  FROM (SELECT DISTINCT user_id, DATE(login_time) AS login_date FROM logins) t
),
streaks AS (
  SELECT user_id, grp, COUNT(*) AS streak_length,
    MIN(login_date) AS start_date, MAX(login_date) AS end_date
  FROM grouped GROUP BY user_id, grp
)
SELECT user_id, MAX(streak_length) AS longest_streak
FROM streaks GROUP BY user_id;
